title: CT-802
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image= |name=CT-802 |homeworld=[[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]{{DB|organization|clonetroopers|clone troopers}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] {{C|[[Cloning/Legends|clone]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]'' |donor=[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] |height=1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] **[[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I don't think he'll see another sunrise, Jos.''"
"''There might be something else we can try.|Barriss Offee and Jos Vondar, on CT-802|}} '''CT-802''' was a [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] who fought for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the [[Battle of Drongar]] in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. During the battle, he was injured and contracted a lethal infection. On the brink of [[Death/Legends|death]], he was [[Transport/Legends|transported]] to the [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7]]. There, he was saved by [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Jos Vondar]], who provided some of the valuable [[Bota/Legends|bota]]—a native Drongar [[Plant/Legends|plant]] with great medicinal qualities—for his treatment, violating the Republic's regulations not to waste bota on [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|I've done what I can, but it isn't enough. I've been keeping him stable by working on him through the Force, but I can't keep that up forever.|Barriss Offee, desperately trying to save CT-802|}} CT-802 was one of the many [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] created on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] to fight in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of the [[MedStar Duology]] in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]]. during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], CT-802 served on the planet [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]], where the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Battle of Drongar|fought]] for control of the planet's fields of [[Bota/Legends|bota]], a highly-valued [[Plant/Legends|plant]] with exceptional medicinal qualities. As the brutal fighting raged on between the two sides, CT-802 was injured{{CSWECite|I|155|CT-802}} and was struck with a [[Death/Legends|deadly]] infection contracted via a local mutated microbe. Unconscious, CT-802 was brought to the [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7]] to undergo treatment. However, the infection was unknown to the RMSU's [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]], and none of their medicine seemed to work, not even [[spectacillin]], which usually helped against most of Drongar's microbe-carried diseases. [[File:Jos_Vondar.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Jos Vondar saved CT-802 by providing some of his bota.]] CT-802's condition worsened with every [[Standard minute/Legends|minute]]: he had a fever that the doctors were barely keeping down with analgesic suppressors and [[Coma/Legends|coma]] induction; his [[Liver/Legends|liver]] was working overtime and was getting tired; his [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Blood/Legends|blood]] cell count was off the charts; fluid began to fill his [[Lung/Legends|lungs]]; and his [[Kidney/Legends|kidneys]] started to shut down. In addition, he had an erratic [[Heart/Legends|heartbeat]] secondary to [[cardiac tamponade]], an emergency condition caused by the accumulation of fluid in the sac that contained the heart. [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], who was stationed at the RMSU, was able to [[Force healing/Legends|stabilize]] the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], but she was not strong enough to sustain him for long. The only thing that could potentially save CT-802 was bota, but the doctors were prohibited from using it, as the Republic had deemed the plant too expensive to waste on clone [[Troop/Legends|troopers]]. However, surgeon [[Jos Vondar]] possessed a secret supply of bota, inherited from his deceased friend and colleague [[Zan Yant]]. Having recently changed his views regarding the value of clone troopers' lives, Vondar was determined to save CT-802. Violating his orders, Vondar provided some of his bota for CT-802's treatment. After an intramuscular injection of bota extract, CT-802 got better. His fever was gone, and his organ systems were on the mend. All in all, the trooper was fully awake and nearly well, although his white cell count was still slightly elevated. ==Personality and traits== Like all of Jango Fett's clones, CT-802 stood 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. ==Behind the scenes== CT-802 first appeared in ''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]'', a [[2004]] novel written by [[Michael Reaves]] and [[Steve Perry]]. CT-802 later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]]. ==Appearances== *''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=CT-802 |fi=CT-802 }} [[Category:Clone troopers]] [[Category:Males]]
